data.body:
PASS-1 review (T5c — Security overlay): PASS / not-applicable, no findings.

This kill-switch/disable-config + rollout-ordering plan adds (1) a project-level fail-open config toggle to disable the gate, (2) a written rollback/back-out + deploy-ordering runbook for the signature/REVIEW_RESULT-sidecar scheme, and (3) a spec cross-reference. It introduces NO new security surface in rebar's domain: no new endpoint or network exposure, no new authn/authz boundary, no new storage/transmission of sensitive data or PII, and no new credential/secret/grant. rebar is a local, git-backed CLI/library; per T5c's not-applicable clause for apps without such a surface, the overlay PASSES.

The signing-key secret lifecycle the signature scheme depends on is PRE-EXISTING and already sound (src/rebar/signing.py: HMAC key resolved from REBAR_SIGNING_KEY or a per-env, gitignored, 0o600 `.signing-key` generated on first use, never committed/shared; client output exposes only the key fingerprint via key_id, never the raw HMAC — see tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_signature.py:292-307). This plan does not touch key custody, add a new grant, or introduce plaintext secrets, so T5c's secret-lifecycle / least-privilege / data-protection checks do not engage as NEW concerns; any secret already in the repo/tickets is an upstream concern, not this review's (ANTI-FP).

Affirmations (T5c): not-applicable — no new endpoint/path or network exposure; no new auth boundary; no new sensitive-data/PII storage or transmission; no new credential/role/grant; no new plaintext-secret handling (existing HMAC key custody is env-var/gitignored-file, fingerprint-only exposure, unchanged by this plan).
